# isotrieve - Embedding Migration Without Re-embedding

> Migrate a vector database to a new embedding model without re-embedding the corpus. Fits a linear map from ~2K calibration texts; 87-91% retrieval retention measured on BEIR.

## Overview

When your embedding model gets deprecated (ada-002 -> text-embedding-3), you face a choice: stay locked to a stale model, or re-embed your entire corpus (expensive in API cost, compute, and ops). Isotrieve offers a third option: fit a linear map between embedding spaces from a small calibration sample, then transform stored vectors locally.

## Install

```bash
pip install isotrieve
```

Python >= 3.10. Core deps: numpy, scikit-learn, typer, rich.

Optional extras:
- `pip install isotrieve[chroma]` - ChromaDB adapter
- `pip install isotrieve[langchain]` - LangChain embeddings shim
- `pip install isotrieve[sentence-transformers]` - local models
- `pip install isotrieve[qdrant]` - Qdrant vector DB
- `pip install isotrieve[pgvector]` - PostgreSQL pgvector
- `pip install isotrieve[openai]` - OpenAI embeddings
- `pip install isotrieve[voyage]` - Voyage embeddings
- `pip install isotrieve[cohere]` - Cohere embeddings
- `pip install isotrieve[gemini]` - Google Gemini embeddings
- `pip install isotrieve[mlp]` - Neural adapter (PyTorch)
- `pip install isotrieve[all]` - Everything

## Quickstart

```python
import numpy as np
from isotrieve import RidgeMapping

# Paired calibration: K texts embedded with both models
rng = np.random.default_rng(0)
K, d_src, d_tgt = 500, 32, 48
X = rng.normal(size=(K, d_src))  # source model embeddings
Y = X @ rng.normal(size=(d_src, d_tgt))  # target model embeddings

# Fit mapping
m = RidgeMapping(alpha="auto", seed=0)
m.fit(X, Y)
m.save("mapping.isotrieve")

# Transform corpus
Z = m.transform(X[:10])  # (10, d_tgt), L2-normalized
```

## CLI

```bash
# Plan migration cost
isotrieve plan --source-model text-embedding-ada-002 \
          --target-model text-embedding-3-large \
          --corpus-size 1000000

# Fit mapping from numpy files
isotrieve calibrate --source-vectors X.npy --target-vectors Y.npy -o map.isotrieve

# Transform corpus
isotrieve transform --mapping map.isotrieve --source-dir ./old_store --target-dir ./new_store

# Inspect mapping
isotrieve inspect map.isotrieve --json
```

## Serve Mode (Zero Corpus Writes)

Map new-model queries into legacy space. No re-embedding, instant rollback:

```python
from isotrieve.serve import QueryAdapter

qa = QueryAdapter.load("mapping.isotrieve")
legacy_vec = qa.map_query(new_model_query_vector)
results = qdrant.search(collection="docs", vector=legacy_vec)
```

## ChromaDB Adapter (v0.2)

### Serve Mode

```python
from isotrieve.adapters.chroma import IsotrieveChromaFunction
from isotrieve.mapping.base import Mapping

mapping = Mapping.load("ada002_to_te3.isotrieve")
ef = IsotrieveChromaFunction(mapping, new_model_embedder=my_embed_fn)
col = client.get_collection("docs", embedding_function=ef)
results = col.query(query_texts=["..."], n_results=10)
```

### Offline Migration

```python
from isotrieve.adapters.chroma import migrate_collection

report = migrate_collection(client, "docs", mapping, new_collection="docs_v2")
print(f"Migrated {report.rows_processed} rows, recall@10={report.sampled_recall_at_10:.3f}")
```

## LangChain Adapter (v0.2)

```python
from isotrieve.adapters.langchain import IsotrieveEmbeddings
from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings

mapping = Mapping.load("ada002_to_te3.isotrieve")
base = OpenAIEmbeddings(model="text-embedding-3-small")
ae = IsotrieveEmbeddings(mapping, base)

# Works with any LangChain vector store
from langchain_chroma import Chroma
db = Chroma.from_documents(docs, embedding=ae)
results = db.similarity_search("query", k=10)
```

## Score Recalibration (v0.2)

Isotonic regression maps cross-space scores to ceiling-equivalent scores:

```python
m = RidgeMapping(alpha="auto", seed=0).fit(X, Y)
m.fit_recalibrator(X_heldout, Y_heldout)  # optional
m.save("mapping.isotrieve")  # recalibrator saved alongside mapping

# At serve time
qa = QueryAdapter.load("mapping.isotrieve")
calibrated_scores = qa.recalibrate_scores(raw_scores)
```

Effect: essential for rectangular pairs (+22% at τ=0.60 for MiniLM→bge). Marginal for same-dim pairs.

## Confidence Scoring (v0.2)

Per-query confidence flags with adaptive P33/P67 margins:

```python
from isotrieve.reranking import ConfidenceScorer

scorer = ConfidenceScorer(margin_high=0.955, margin_low=0.637)
result = scorer.score(query_vector, top_scores)
print(result.flag)  # "high", "medium", or "low"
```

Predictive across both pairs: high-conf R@10=0.955 (bge→e5), 0.875 (MiniLM→bge).

## Quality Gate

The gate predicts retrieval retention from holdout proxies using isotonic regression:

```python
from isotrieve.quality.gate import QualityGate

gate = QualityGate()
report = gate.evaluate(m, X_holdout, Y_holdout)

print(report.verdict)  # PASS / WARN / FAIL
print(report.predicted_retention)  # e.g., 0.838
print(report.prediction_interval)  # e.g., (0.767, 0.908)
```

Gate model: `src/isotrieve/quality/gate_model_v1.json` (trained on local model pairs only).

## Adapters

| Adapter | Retention | Notes |
|---------|----------|-------|
| Ridge | 0.866 +/- 0.008 | Default. Fast, stable. |
| LowRank | 0.857 +/- 0.009 | Compressed matrix. ~1% worse. |
| Procrustes | 0.851 +/- 0.007 | Square only. Orthogonal rotation. |
| MLP | 0.719 +/- 0.008 | No tuning. Linear wins. |

Ridge is recommended for all cases. Use LowRank for compressed storage. MLP is not recommended.

## Benchmarks

All numbers from `benchmarks/results/`, verified by `benchmarks/audit_configs.py`.

### K-sweep (all adapters averaged, SciFact, 3 seeds)

| K | Retention | Gate |
|---|----------|------|
| 500 | 0.667 +/- 0.039 | WARN |
| 1000 | 0.732 +/- 0.056 | WARN |
| 2000 | 0.788 +/- 0.054 | PASS |
| 4000 | 0.814 +/- 0.068 | PASS |

### Same-dim pair (bge-large->e5-large, 1024->1024)

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Floor (raw cross-space) | 0.0 |
| Isotrieve (mapped) | 0.656 |
| Ceiling (full re-embed) | 0.722 |
| Retention | 0.908 |

Same dimension != same space. e5 models require "query: "/"passage: " prefixes.

### Score Recalibration

| Pair | MAE | Margin Compression | Notes |
|------|-----|-------------------|-------|
| bge->e5 (same-dim) | 0.095 | 0.83x | Marginal improvement |
| MiniLM->bge (rectangular) | 0.364 | 0.85x | Essential (+22% at τ=0.60) |

### Confidence Flags

| Pair | High-conf R@10 | Low-conf R@10 | Gap |
|------|---------------|---------------|-----|
| bge->e5 | 0.955 | 0.637 | 0.318 |
| MiniLM->bge | 0.875 | 0.651 | 0.224 |

### Null Results (logged, not hidden)

- Cross-encoder reranking: −10.7pts (bge→e5), −9.8pts (MiniLM→bge). MS MARCO model domain-mismatched.
- TSVD shrinkage: rank=512 only −0.33pt. Not worth complexity.
- Procrustes centering: −55pt on unit vectors. Reverted (breaks serve-mode).

## When NOT to use

- Maximum retrieval quality matters more than cost -> re-embed
- Calibration domain mismatches corpus
- Quality gate returns FAIL
- K < 2000 (quality degrades significantly)
- You need unsupervised migration (Isotrieve requires paired calibration)

## Anti-patterns

- Do not mix vectors from different models in one collection
- Do not assume same dimensionality means compatibility
- Do not skip the quality gate
- Do not use MLP adapter (0.719 vs 0.866 for Ridge)
- Do not use Procrustes centering on unit vectors (breaks serve-mode)

## How it works

1. Embed K texts with source and target models -> matrices X, Y
2. Fit ridge map Y = [X | 1] W (handles unequal dims)
3. Hold out 10% to estimate quality
4. Transform corpus: V' = normalize(V @ W) (streaming batches)
5. Write to new collection; keep old as rollback

## Prior art

Engineering, not research. Built on:
- vec2vec (Jha et al., 2025)
- Drift-Adapter (EMNLP 2025)
- Platonic Representation Hypothesis (Huh et al., 2024)

## Security

Embedding translation enables inversion-style attacks. Treat mapped vectors with same sensitivity as source text.
